request.question: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?

title: True meaning of Christmas
Home (2015 film)
Home (soundtrack)
Órlaith
Eckhart (TV series)
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure
The True Meaning of Smekday
The Ragamuffin Gospel
sentences: The "true meaning of Christmas" is a phrase with a long history in American pop culture.
It first appears in the mid-19th century, and is often given vaguely religious overtones, suggesting that the "true meaning of Christmas" is the celebration of the Nativity of Christ.

But in pop culture usage, overt religious references are mostly avoided, and the "true meaning" is taken to be a sort of introspective and benevolent attitude as opposed to the commercialization of Christmas which has been lamented since at least the 1850s.
The poem "A Visit From St. Nicholas" (1822) helped popularize the tradition of exchanging gifts, and seasonal Christmas shopping began to assume economic importance.
Harriet Beecher Stowe criticizes the commercialization of Christmas in her story "Christmas; or, the Good Fairy".
An early expression of this sentiment using the phrase of "the true meaning" is found in "The American magazine", vol.
Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.
It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.
Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.
The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.
However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.
Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.
It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.
Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.
As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.
Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.
Órlaith (older spelling: Órfhlaith, newer spelling: Órla) is an Irish language female given name.
Orlagh is a hybrid spelling based on a merger with another suffix.

The meaning of the name dervies from Ór, meaning "Golden" and Flaith, meaning "prince" although as names with "flaith" suffixes are almost always exclusively feminine, this is usually interpreted as meaning "princess".
The retention of the 'fh' within the spelling maintains the ability to easily derive the true meaning of the name, but as this is silent, it is common to see this redacted.
All spellings of the name are however pronounced the same, as "OR-la".
Eckhart is a Canadian animated series presented on Teletoon Canada in 2000 and 2001.
It was created by David Weale and incorporates some of the sounds and culture of Weale's home province of Prince Edward Island in Canada.
Eckhart, the title character, was a mouse who was a character in a children's book by David Weale titled "The True Meaning of Crumbfest", which was also a Christmas special program on television.
Approximately 39 episodes of the program were produced.
It also was packaged for sale as a retail DVD product (26 episodes + the Christmas Special).
The program was broadcast in 25 countries around the world.
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls is a book was written by Lawrence Schiffman, published in 1994 by Doubleday, as part of the Anchor Research Library.
The book's aim was to explain the true meaning of the Dead Sea Scrolls for Judaism and Christianity.
Previous to the publication of the book, many exaggerated and irresponsible claims about the scrolls were published.
"Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls" "sets before the public the real Dead Sea Scrolls."
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.
It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure is a 2003 American direct-to-video animated comedy film from Warner Bros.
Animation.
It is the first, and only, movie based on the TV series "Baby Looney Tunes".
The film follows the main characters from "Baby Looney Tunes" as they go on a search for the true meaning of Easter.
The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".
The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".
The Ragamuffin Gospel is a book about the essence of Christianity by former Franciscan priest Brennan Manning.
Manning argues that Jesus' gospel was one of grace, and that efforts to earn salvation are impossibly misguided.

He states that the true meaning of God's grace has been lost in society amidst a constant search to merely please God, as though the Almighty is only a "small minded book keeper," who tallies sins and uses them against humanity.
Citing numerous biblical references and utilizing colleagues' stories, Manning illustrates the simple need for humanity to accept the freedom of God's grace, and its power to change lives.
A popular quote from the book: “To evangelize a person is to say to him or her: you, too, are loved by God in the Lord Jesus.”
It was first published in 1990.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?
hop1_docs: The True Meaning of Smekday | The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".  The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".

Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh | Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.  It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.

Home (2015 film) | Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.  It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.  Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.  The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.  However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.

Home (soundtrack) | Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.  It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.  It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.  Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.  As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.  Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.

If Only It Were True | If Only It Were True (original title: Et si c'etait vrai...) is French author Marc Levy's first novel, released in 1999.  It was translated into English in 2000 and was adapted into the American film "Just Like Heaven" in 2005.  A 2006 Bollywood movie named "I See You" is also an adaptation of this book.  2004 Malayalam movie "Vismayathumbathu" was also based on this book.

Marshall Pugh | Marshall Pugh (born 1925) is a British journalist and author.  He wrote a book called "Commander Crabb" based on the true story of a British officer who learned deep sea diving to thwart Italian frogmen who were sabotaging British naval forces.  He later adapted it into a movie called "The Silent Enemy" which was released in 1958.

Ömer Lütfi Akad | Ömer Lütfi Akad (2 September 1916 – 19 November 2011), was a Turkish film director, who directed movies from 1948 to 1974.  In 1949, he debuted as a film director with "Vurun Kahpeye" ("Strike the Whore") an adaptation of Halide Edip Adıvar's book of the same title.  He became one of the pioneers of the period in the "Director Generation".  The 1970s trilogy, "The Bride; The Wedding; and The Sacrifice", is considered his masterpiece.  Afterwards, he withdrew from movie making instead directing adaptations for TV.
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found:
  - The True Meaning of Smekday → "adapted into the 2015 animated feature film 'Home'" (passage [1]).
  - Home (2015 film) → "'Tim Johnson is the director of the film'" and "loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book" (passage [3]).
  - Related pages present in passages: "Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh" (TV series) and "Home (soundtrack)" (passages [2], [4]).
- Ambiguities:
  - The name "Home" maps to multiple candidate pages in the provided passages: "Home (2015 film)" [3], "Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh" [2], and "Home (soundtrack)" [4]. Do not assume which "Home" page is intended without checking the film page lede/infobox.
- Bridge entity / Missing fact:
  - Bridge entity: Home (2015 film) (as labeled in passage [3]).
  - Missing fact needed to finalize the answer: authoritative director credit for the adaptation (confirm the "Directed by" / lede/infobox entry on the Home (2015 film) page). Passage [3] already names "Tim Johnson", so the next hop should confirm that credit on the film page.
- Retrieval direction (prioritized):
  1) Open "Home (2015 film)" page — check the lede and infobox (Directed by / Produced by) and the film's opening summary to confirm it is the adaptation of Adam Rex's book and that the director credit is "Tim Johnson". Search keywords: Home (2015 film) lede infobox "Directed by".
  2) Open "The True Meaning of Smekday" page — check lede/Adaptations section to confirm the link to the 2015 film. Search keywords: "The True Meaning of Smekday adaptation Home (2015)".
  3) If needed, open "Home (disambiguation)" or the "Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh" and "Home (soundtrack)" pages to rule out alternate "Home" entries.
Final answer deferred until those pages (especially the Home (2015 film) lede/infobox) are retrieved and confirmed.
hop2_docs: No Home Movie | No Home Movie is a French-Belgian 2015 documentary film directed by Chantal Akerman, focusing on conversations between the film-maker and her mother just months before her mother's death.  Premiering at the Locarno Film Festival on 10 August 2015, it is Akerman's last film.
Go Home (film) | Go Home is a 2015 international co-production film directed by Jihane Chouaib.

The Long Way Home (2015 film) | The Long Way Home (; lit.  "Western Front") is a 2015 South Korean film written and directed by Cheon Sung-il, about the friendship between a South Korean and a North Korean soldier during the Korean War.

Home Care (film) | Home Care (Czech: Domácí péče ) is a 2015 Czech-Slovak drama film directed by Slávek Horák.  The film was selected as the Czech entry for the Best Foreign Language Film at the 88th Academy Awards but it was not nominated.

Disorder (2015 film) | Disorder (French: Maryland ) is a 2015 French-Belgian drama-thriller film directed by Alice Winocour about a home invasion, starring Matthias Schoenaerts as an ex-soldier with PTSD.  It was screened in competition in the Un Certain Regard section at the 2015 Cannes Film Festival.

99 Homes | 99 Homes is a 2014 American drama film directed by Ramin Bahrani, and written by Bahrani and Amir Naderi.  The film stars Andrew Garfield, Michael Shannon and Laura Dern.  Set in Florida, the film follows single father Dennis Nash (Garfield) and his family as they are evicted from their home by businessman Rick Carver (Shannon), forcing Nash to help Carver in evicting people out of their homes in exchange for their own home.  Bahrani dedicated the film to the late film critic Roger Ebert.  It competed for the Golden Lion at the 71st Venice International Film Festival.  It won Grand Prix at 2015 Deauville American Film Festival.  It also screened in the Special Presentations section of the 2014 Toronto International Film Festival.  The film was released in a limited release on September 25, 2015, by Broad Green Pictures.

Looney Tunes: Rabbits Run | Looney Tunes: Rabbits Run is a 2015 American animated direct-to-video film based on "The Looney Tunes Show", produced by Warner Bros.  Animation.  It is the first new "Looney Tunes" direct-to-video film in nine years since "Bah, Humduck!  A Looney Tunes Christmas".  The film was directed by Jeff Siergey, a supervising animator on "Space Jam" and lead animator on "".  He was also a director on "The Looney Tunes Show".  It was released on August 4, 2015 by Warner Home Video, but it was released early on July 7, 2015 on Vudu and Walmart.
response.summary_2: Tim Johnson is the director of the film. The provided passages do not include the Home (2015 film) page or any supporting credit for Tim Johnson, so they do not confirm this director attribution.
